machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ATPase (energiser; substrate translocation / pilus assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: Some of the components of Cag-T4SS share homology to that of VirB proteins including putative energy providing CagE (HP0544), the largest VirB4 homologue.
machinery_genes.note: Shariq 2015 biochemical characterization of CagE in H. pylori cag-T4SS; CagE = HP0544 in 26695 (locus_tag for note only). Shown to be an inner-membrane-associated active NTPase interacting with CagV and Cagbeta.

machinery_genes.quote: These include Cagγ (VirB1), CagC (VirB2), CagL (VirB5), CagW (VirB6), CagT (VirB7) and CagV (VirB8)
machinery_genes.note: Shariq 2015 names CagW as the VirB6 homologue of the H. pylori Cag-T4SS (HP0540 in 26695).

machinery_genes.quote: These include Cagγ (VirB1), CagC (VirB2), CagL (VirB5), CagW (VirB6), CagT (VirB7) and CagV (VirB8)
machinery_genes.note: Shariq 2015 names CagV as the VirB8 homologue (HP0538 in 26695); demonstrated to directly interact with the VirB4 ATPase CagE.

machinery_genes.quote: We show that this complex contains five H. pylori proteins, CagM, CagT, Cag3, CagX, and CagY, each of which is required for cag T4SS activity. CagX and CagY are orthologous to the VirB9 and VirB10 components of T4SSs in other bacterial species
machinery_genes.note: Frick-Cheng 2016 core-complex isolation; CagX = HP0528 (Table 1, gene number). VirB9 ortholog.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane to outer-membrane channel ring (core complex)
machinery_genes.quote: CagX and CagY are orthologous to the VirB9 and VirB10 components of T4SSs in other bacterial species
machinery_genes.note: Frick-Cheng 2016 core-complex paper; CagY = HP0527 (Table 1). VirB10 ortholog; spans the membrane-crossing channel.

machinery_genes.quote: We show that this complex contains five H. pylori proteins, CagM, CagT, Cag3, CagX, and CagY, each of which is required for cag T4SS activity.
machinery_genes.note: Frick-Cheng 2016; CagT = HP0532 (Table 1). Independently named a VirB7 homologue/lipoprotein: Shariq 2015 (PMID 26565397) lists 'CagT (VirB7)'. Required for organized outer ring.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane core-complex component (cap; required for complex assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: We show that this complex contains five H. pylori proteins, CagM, CagT, Cag3, CagX, and CagY, each of which is required for cag T4SS activity... the other three Cag proteins are unique to H. pylori.
machinery_genes.note: Frick-Cheng 2016; CagM = HP0537 (Table 1). Species-specific (no VirB homolog) but a bona fide structural component of the core complex; CagM deletion abolishes ring assembly.

machinery_genes.quote: Immunogold labeling studies confirm that Cag3 is a peripheral component of the complex.
machinery_genes.note: Frick-Cheng 2016; Cag3 = HP0522 (Table 1). Species-specific peripheral component required for cag T4SS activity; localizes to the periphery of the outer ring.

machinery_genes.quote: Secretion of CagA crucially depends on the hexameric ATPase, HP0525, a member of the VirB11-PulE family.
machinery_genes.note: Yeo 2000 crystal structure of the H. pylori T4SS VirB11 ATPase HP0525 (also called CagAlpha / Cag-alpha). Gene name HP0525 recorded as the name used in the literature; VirB11 family energiser.

machinery_genes.quote: Stability of the inner membrane associated energetic component Cagβ, a VirD4 homologue seems to be partially affected in its absence.
machinery_genes.note: Shariq 2015 names Cagbeta as the VirD4 homologue (coupling protein) of the Cag-T4SS = HP0524. Independently, Fischer 2001 (PMID 11886563) names 'hp0524 (virD4 homologue)' as absolutely essential for CagA translocation.

machinery_genes.quote: These include Cagγ (VirB1), CagC (VirB2), CagL (VirB5), CagW (VirB6), CagT (VirB7) and CagV (VirB8)
machinery_genes.note: Shariq 2015 names CagC as the VirB2 (major pilin) homologue of the Cag-T4SS.

machinery_genes.quote: a specialized adhesin on the pilus surface, the protein CagL, a putative VirB5 ortholog, which binds to host cell β(1) integrin, triggering subsequent delivery of CagA across the host cell membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Conradi 2012 names CagL as a putative VirB5 ortholog and pilus-surface adhesin (HP0539). Also listed as 'CagL (VirB5)' in Shariq 2015 (PMID 26565397). A structural pilus component of the apparatus, not the translocated effector.

machinery_genes.role: lytic transglycosylase (cell-wall remodelling for apparatus assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: These include Cagγ (VirB1), CagC (VirB2), CagL (VirB5), CagW (VirB6), CagT (VirB7) and CagV (VirB8)
machinery_genes.note: Shariq 2015 names Caggamma (Cag-gamma) as the VirB1 homologue. Lytic transglycosylase role from VirB1 family in the apparatus brief; lower-confidence functional assignment (named as homolog, not biochemically characterized in this organism here).

notes: This is the H. pylori cag pathogenicity island (cag-PAI) type-IVA secretion system, the archetypal Cag T4SS that injects the CagA oncoprotein into gastric epithelial cells. All work is on H. pylori 26695, the type strain whose cag-PAI defined the HP locus numbering (Censini 1996, Fischer 2001), so no cross-strain transfer was needed; supporting structural/biochemical papers (Yeo 2000 HP0525, Shariq 2015 CagE) are also on H. pylori. Core apparatus covered: VirB orthologs CagE/VirB4 (ATPase), CagW/VirB6, CagV/VirB8, CagX/VirB9, CagY/VirB10, CagT/VirB7, HP0525-CagAlpha/VirB11 (ATPase), Cagbeta-HP0524/VirD4 (coupling protein), CagC/VirB2 (major pilin), CagL/VirB5 (pilus adhesin), CagGamma/VirB1; plus species-specific structural core-complex components CagM and Cag3 (no VirB ortholog but required for the membrane-spanning core complex; Frick-Cheng 2016). Gene field uses the literature name; HP locus_tags are in notes only (resolution is a later scripted step). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: CagA (HP0547, the translocated effector / this instance's curated effector); CagF (HP0543, the CagA chaperone, copurifies with the core complex but Frick-Cheng 2016 show CagF/CagA are not integral core-complex components); pilus-associated accessory proteins CagH/CagI/CagD and CagZ (named in Shariq 2015 as Cag-specific factors, several non-essential per Fischer 2001 hp0520/0521/0534/0535/0536; not counted as core channel apparatus to avoid padding). LOW-CONFIDENCE call: CagGamma/VirB1 is named as a homolog in Shariq 2015 but its lytic-transglycosylase function was not biochemically demonstrated in these sources. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PMC full texts / abstracts; every DOI and PMID was verified via get_article_metadata.
